name: Alert Rule Builder
description: Visual condition builder for creating alert rules, filters, or automation triggers using nested AND/OR logic groups with metric selectors, operators, threshold sliders, and live rule testing.

standard.description: Condition groups with AND/OR logic toggles between rows — supports one level of nesting with groups rendered as bordered cards containing their conditions

simple.description: Single flat list of conditions joined by a single logic operator — no nesting or groups, ideal for basic threshold alerts with just one or two conditions

advanced.description: Deeply nested AND/OR tree with unlimited group depth — groups can contain sub-groups creating a visual tree structure with indentation and connecting lines for complex rule logic

visual_brief: Card-based rule builder with indented groups representing AND/OR logic. The top level is a bordered card (the root group) containing condition rows stacked vertically. Each condition row is a horizontal strip with four inline elements: a metric dropdown selector showing the metric name (e.g., 'CPU Usage', 'Error Rate', 'Latency p99'), an operator dropdown (>, <, =, !=, contains, between), a value input field (numeric or text depending on metric type), and a small circular remove button with an X icon at the right edge. Between each condition row sits a small pill-shaped logic toggle button showing 'AND' or 'OR' in uppercase — clicking toggles between the two. The pill color is blue for AND and orange for OR. Nested groups appear as indented bordered cards within the parent group, with a colored left border matching their logic operator color. An 'Add condition' button with a plus icon sits at the bottom of each group, alongside an 'Add group' button for nesting. The ThresholdSlider variant replaces the plain value input with a slider overlaid on a mini sparkline chart of recent metric data, with a draggable horizontal line showing the threshold position. A prominent 'Test Rule' button at the bottom evaluates the complete rule against recent data and shows a pass (green checkmark) or fail (red X) result with a count of matching data points. The entire builder has a subtle background tint distinguishing it from surrounding content.

slots.rule-header: Top bar showing the rule name as an editable text field, a severity selector (info/warning/critical) as colored radio pills, and a notification channel selector dropdown. The severity pills are blue for info, amber for warning, and red for critical.
slots.rule-group: Repeating slot — the primary structural unit. A bordered card with rounded corners and a colored left border (blue for AND group, orange for OR group). Contains a small group-level logic label in the top-left corner ('ALL of' for AND, 'ANY of' for OR). Inside: a vertical stack of ConditionRow slots separated by logic toggle pills, followed by action buttons at the bottom.
slots.condition-row: Repeating slot within a rule-group. A horizontal row containing: MetricSelector (dropdown, 180px width, shows metric name with a small type icon), OperatorSelector (dropdown, 100px width, shows operator symbol), ValueInput (text/number input, 120px width) or ThresholdSlider (240px width slider on mini-chart), and a remove button (circular, 24px, muted until hovered). The row has a subtle hover background. Drag handle on the left edge for reordering.
slots.logic-toggle: Small pill button (60px wide, 24px tall) centered between condition rows. Shows 'AND' or 'OR' text. Clicking toggles the logic operator for the containing group. Background color matches the operator: blue for AND, orange for OR. Smooth color transition on toggle.
slots.threshold-slider: An enhanced value input variant. Shows a mini sparkline chart (240px wide, 48px tall) of recent metric data with a horizontal draggable threshold line. The area above or below the line (depending on operator direction) tints in the severity color. A numeric readout appears above the drag handle. Moving the handle updates the value input in real time.
slots.test-section: Bottom area containing the Test button and result display. The 'Test Rule' button is prominent with a play icon. When clicked, it shows a loading spinner, then displays results: a pass/fail badge, the number of data points that matched out of total evaluated, and a small timeline showing when matches occurred in the recent window.

responsive.mobile: Condition rows stack vertically — metric selector, operator, and value input each take full width on separate lines. Logic toggles remain between conditions. Nested groups indent with a thinner left border. Threshold slider stretches to full width. Test button is sticky at the bottom of the viewport.
responsive.tablet: Condition rows display horizontally with slightly compressed widths. Groups maintain full indentation. Test section is visible without scrolling on most rules.
responsive.desktop: Full horizontal condition rows with generous spacing. Threshold sliders show wider mini-charts. Drag-to-reorder is smooth with drop zone indicators. Multiple levels of nesting display cleanly with indentation guides.

micro.toggle-flip: background-color + text transition 200ms ease-out on logic toggle
micro.remove-hover: scale(1→1.1) + color shift to red 150ms ease-out

ambient.threshold-glow: subtle pulse on threshold line when value is near current metric reading

layout_hints.rule_priority: The user should be able to read rule logic top to bottom before thinking about nested complexity. Group hierarchy and AND/OR relationships must remain unmistakable.
layout_hints.edit_focus: Row-level controls should support editing without overpowering the logic structure. Add/remove actions belong at the edge of the builder, not in the center of attention.
layout_hints.test_feedback: Rule testing should feel attached to the current rule state, not like a separate diagnostics panel detached from the builder.

composition.RuleGroup: Card(d-surface, bordered, indent: depth, color-left-border: logic) > [GroupLabel(d-annotation) + ConditionRow*(d-interactive) + LogicToggle*(d-interactive) + ActionButtons > [AddConditionButton + AddGroupButton]]
composition.ConditionRow: Row(d-interactive) > [DragHandle + MetricSelector(d-control) + OperatorSelector(d-control) + ValueInput(d-control) | ThresholdSlider(d-interactive) + RemoveButton]

keyboard: Tab: move between condition row inputs and controls
Enter: confirm dropdown selection or trigger test
Arrow Up/Down: navigate dropdown options in metric and operator selectors
Delete/Backspace: remove focused condition row (with confirmation)
Ctrl+G: add a new nested group
Escape: close open dropdowns or cancel pending removal
announcements: Rule test result: {pass|fail}, {matchCount} of {total} data points matched
